text:
WHOLE-BODY FDG PET/CT SCAN

CLINICAL INDICATION:
66-year-old female with recently diagnosed pancreatic head/uncinate process adenocarcinoma (EUS-guided biopsy confirmed, moderately differentiated). Multiphasic CT abdomen/pelvis performed at the time of initial evaluation demonstrated a 3.8 x 3.2 cm hypoenhancing mass in the pancreatic head/uncinate region with superior mesenteric artery (SMA) abutment exceeding 180 degrees of circumference and superior mesenteric vein (SMV) involvement with partial luminal narrowing. One peripancreatic lymph node at 1.2 cm noted as suspicious. CA 19-9 markedly elevated at 1,247 U/mL. PET/CT requested for comprehensive metabolic staging prior to initiation of treatment, to evaluate for occult distant metastatic disease not detected on diagnostic CT.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body integrated FDG PET/CT was performed following intravenous administration of 12.4 mCi (459 MBq) of 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose. Patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to radiopharmaceutical injection. Serum glucose at the time of injection was 138 mg/dL (patient has known type 2 diabetes mellitus; no insulin administered within 4 hours of injection; exam proceeded per institutional protocol). Uptake period: 62 minutes. Imaging was performed from the vertex of the skull to mid-thigh using standard time-of-flight PET acquisition with low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic co-registration. Diagnostic quality CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with intravenous contrast as part of this examination (100 mL Omnipaque 350, power injected). Slice thickness: 3 mm reconstructed. Iterative reconstruction algorithm applied. All images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. SUV measurements represent standardized uptake values corrected for lean body mass (SUL) and body weight (SUVmax). Background liver SUVmean: 2.6.

FINDINGS:

HEAD, NECK, AND BRAIN:
No intracranial FDG-avid lesions. Normal cortical distribution of FDG. No hypermetabolic cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. No parotid or submandibular gland uptake above physiologic background. No orbital or nasal cavity abnormalities. Thyroid gland demonstrates diffuse, mild, symmetric FDG uptake consistent with physiologic or low-grade thyroiditis pattern; no discrete hypermetabolic nodule identified. No focal osseous lesions in the calvarium or skull base.

CHEST:
Lungs: No pulmonary nodules or masses with abnormal FDG uptake. Several small bilateral pulmonary micronodules are noted on the CT component (largest 4 mm, right lower lobe), all below the threshold for reliable FDG avidity at this resolution; these are nonspecific and likely inflammatory or granulomatous in etiology. No lobar or segmental consolidation with abnormal metabolic activity. No pleural effusions. Pleura: No pleural nodularity or FDG-avid pleural implants.
Cardiomediastinum: Cardiac chambers demonstrate physiologic FDG distribution. No pericardial FDG avidity. Aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber and without abnormal uptake. Mediastinal contour unremarkable.
Lymph nodes: No enlarged or hypermetabolic mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes. Several small subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes are present (largest 7 mm in the right paratracheal station) and are non-FDG-avid, consistent with reactive or incidental nodes.
Bones (thoracic): No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions with associated FDG uptake in the thoracic spine, ribs, sternum, or clavicles. No pathologic fractures.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
Pancreas - Primary Tumor: There is a conspicuous, intensely hypermetabolic soft tissue mass in the head and uncinate process of the pancreas. This lesion demonstrates markedly elevated FDG avidity with an SUVmax of 8.9 (background liver SUVmean 2.6; lesion-to-liver ratio approximately 3.4). On the co-registered CT, the mass measures approximately 3.8 x 3.2 cm, appearing hypoenhancing relative to the surrounding pancreatic parenchyma with an ill-defined margin and surrounding desmoplastic reaction. Extensive contact of the mass with the origin of the SMA is again confirmed on the CT component, with the tumor appearing to encircle greater than 180 degrees of the vessel circumference at the level of its takeoff. The SMV at the level of the pancreatic head appears narrowed at its confluence, with soft tissue abutment. Main pancreatic duct is moderately dilated, consistent with the previously described upstream ductal obstruction. The pancreatic body and tail show mild parenchymal atrophy upstream of the obstructing mass without abnormal FDG uptake to suggest secondary malignant involvement.
Liver: No hepatic lesions demonstrating abnormal FDG uptake above hepatic background. No discrete hepatic hypodensities or masses on the CT component. The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal hepatic arterial or portal phase enhancement abnormality. No biliary ductal dilatation at the level of the hepatic hilum, though mild intrahepatic biliary prominence is present in the right hepatic lobe consistent with the known downstream biliary obstruction. The common bile duct measures 12 mm in maximal diameter on the CT component, consistent with prior evaluation.
Gallbladder: Gallbladder is present and non-distended. No cholelithiasis or cholecystitis. No FDG uptake in the gallbladder wall above background.
Spleen: Normal size and homogeneous uptake. No splenic lesions. No splenomegaly.
Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No adrenal nodules or masses. No FDG-avid adrenal lesions. Small bilateral adrenal limbs are present without thickening or nodularity.
Kidneys: Normal bilateral renal uptake with physiologic tracer excretion into the collecting systems and ureters. No renal masses with abnormal FDG avidity. No hydronephrosis.
Stomach: Gastric wall demonstrates physiologic FDG uptake without focal hypermetabolic lesion. No evidence of gastric involvement.

LYMPH NODES - REGIONAL:
There is a single peripancreatic lymph node in the periportal/hepatoduodenal ligament region that demonstrates mildly elevated FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 4.2. On the CT component, this node measures approximately 1.2 cm in short axis and demonstrates soft tissue density without calcification. This degree of metabolic activity in this location in the setting of a known pancreatic head primary is suspicious for nodal metastasis, although reactive nodal enlargement cannot be entirely excluded at this level of avidity.
No additional regional lymph nodes in the celiac axis, superior mesenteric, para-aortic, or aortocaval chains demonstrate FDG avidity exceeding background or are enlarged beyond 1 cm in short axis on CT.

LOWER ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Small and large bowel: Normal physiologic FDG distribution. No focal bowel wall uptake concerning for malignant involvement. No bowel wall thickening on CT.
Peritoneum and mesentery: No peritoneal surface nodularity or plaque-like FDG avidity to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis. The omentum is without fatty infiltration or nodular deposits. No free ascites identified. Mesenteric fat is clear.
Pelvic organs: Uterus is present, age-appropriately atrophic, without abnormal FDG uptake. No adnexal lesions. Urinary bladder demonstrates physiologic tracer excretion. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No presacral or iliac chain FDG-avid nodes.
Pelvic bones: No FDG-avid osseous lesions in the pelvis, sacrum, or proximal femora.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
No hypermetabolic osseous lesions in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton to suggest bone metastases. No lytic or sclerotic lesions on CT with associated abnormal FDG activity. Mild degenerative changes in the lumbar spine and bilateral hip joints without metabolic activity above background, consistent with spondylosis.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous or deep soft tissue FDG-avid lesions. No evidence of superficial nodal involvement.

IMPRESSION:

1. Intensely hypermetabolic mass in the pancreatic head and uncinate process (SUVmax 8.9), consistent with the known primary pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. The metabolic footprint corresponds anatomically to the mass identified on prior multiphasic CT, with extensive SMA encasement and SMV involvement as previously characterized.

2. Single FDG-avid peripancreatic lymph node in the periportal region (SUVmax 4.2), morphologically enlarged at 1.2 cm, suspicious for locoregional nodal involvement. This is consistent with the finding on the prior diagnostic CT.

3. No hepatic FDG-avid lesions identified. The liver is clear of metabolically active deposits at this examination.

4. No peritoneal FDG avidity. No CT evidence of peritoneal implants or ascites. Peritoneal surface disease is not demonstrated.

5. No FDG-avid pulmonary nodules or masses. The small bilateral micronodules are below the reliable threshold for PET assessment and are nonspecific.

6. No FDG-avid osseous lesions to suggest skeletal metastases.

7. No FDG-avid distant lymphadenopathy in the mediastinum, retroperitoneum, or pelvis.

Overall, the findings are consistent with locoregional pancreatic adenocarcinoma with regional nodal involvement and no definitive evidence of distant metastatic disease on this whole-body FDG PET/CT examination. The primary tumor's metabolic profile is highly consistent with active, metabolically aggressive disease confined to the pancreatic head region and regional lymphatics at this time. These findings support further evaluation and treatment planning in the multidisciplinary oncologic setting.
